body.title: Verify confab_scanner wiring in ConsultSession — 0/104 flags is suspicious
body.proposal: Igor wants to edit wild_igor/igor/brainstem/core_patterns.py (HIGH inertia). Plan: . Reason: HIGH inertia write requires human approval: wild_igor/igor/brainstem/core_patterns.py
body.description:
Consult observe-and-tune memo (lab/design_docs/consult_tuning_2026-04-23.md Finding 5): 0/104 confab_flag events across 104 sessions. Possibilities: (a) scanner too conservative, (b) model outputs don't confabulate enough, (c) scanner not actually wired to the raw_text path. (c) is the scariest — wiring broken means we're flying blind on confab in consults.

Fix: use the engram-engineering toolkit I just built to validate itself. deposit_engram a known-confabulation REFERENCE (temporal drift or capability confab), run a synthetic consult whose raw_text contains a tell-phrase, verify confab_flag fires. If it doesn't fire, trace why — ConsultSession.ask() was supposed to scan raw_text pre-TWM-integration (T-consult-confab-scan done 2026-04-23).

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/cognition/consult.py (ConsultSession.ask), lab/claudecode/engram_tools/confab_scanner.py, lab/claudecode/engram_tools/deposit_engram.py, lab/claudecode/engram_tools/verify_retrieval.py, tests/test_consult_confab_scan.py (add wiring-verify test).
**Design rules:** docs-in-code, test-plan-or-why-not.
**Scope boundary:** IN — prove or disprove wiring via test + fix if broken. OUT — re-tuning confab_scanner thresholds (separate finding).
**Test plan:** new test deposits known confab probe, runs consult with tell-phrase raw_text, asserts confab_flags non-empty. Existing 8 test_consult_confab_scan tests still pass.
